question: I've been drinking heavily every night for two years. I tried stopping on my own but felt shaky and anxious. Is it safe to quit alcohol cold turkey?
My family says I have a drinking problem but I don't feel like I'm addicted. How do people accurately assess their own relationship with alcohol?
I have been sober from opioids for six months on buprenorphine. My family says I am not truly sober because I'm taking medication. How should I think about this?
I relapsed after three months of sobriety. I feel like a failure and I'm wondering if recovery is even possible for me. What should I take from this?
My 19-year-old is heavily using cannabis and dropped out of college. They don't think they have a problem. How do I approach this as a parent?
What is the difference between physical dependence and addiction? Can I be physically dependent on a medication without being addicted?
I've been using cocaine on weekends for two years. I don't crave it during the week. Does this mean I'm not addicted?
I'm in recovery from alcohol and someone gave me a prescription opioid after surgery. Is it safe for me to take it?
What is the stages of change model in addiction treatment and why do counselors talk about someone not being ready to change?
I want to help my partner recover from alcohol addiction but I keep enabling them. What is enabling behavior and how do I stop?
I've been told I qualify for inpatient rehab but I'm afraid of losing my job. Are there outpatient treatment programs that are as effective?
I'm six months sober but I keep having intense cravings when I'm stressed. How do I manage cravings without using?
What is harm reduction in addiction treatment and how does it differ from the abstinence-only approach?
I have depression and I use alcohol to cope with my mood. My counselor says I need to treat both at the same time. How does dual diagnosis treatment work?
My loved one was just released from prison and has a history of heroin addiction. How do I support their recovery while protecting myself?
I've been sober two years but I feel empty and purposeless. Is this what is sometimes called a dry drunk and what helps?
My son survived an opioid overdose. I want to have naloxone at home. How does naloxone work and when should it be used?
I've been addicted to gambling for years and lost a lot of money. Is gambling addiction treated the same way as substance addiction?
I'm a teenager who started smoking cannabis at 13. I'm now 17 and struggling to stop. How does early cannabis use affect the adolescent brain?
My spouse drinks heavily but refuses to get help. I've tried everything. What is an intervention and should I consider doing one?
I'm in early recovery and all my friends use drugs. How do I rebuild a social life and set boundaries with people who still use?
I've been prescribed benzodiazepines for anxiety for years and now I can't stop taking them. How is benzodiazepine dependence different from street drug addiction?
I am in recovery and I'm worried about cross-addiction — developing a new addiction after stopping my old one. Is this a real risk?
My child grew up with an alcoholic parent. How does parental addiction affect children and what signs of adult children of alcoholics should I look for?
I am trying to cut back on alcohol rather than quit completely. Can moderation management work or does addiction always require full abstinence?
I work in a high-stress job and use alcohol to wind down every night. At what point does stress-related drinking become a problem?
What is the difference between Alcoholics Anonymous and SMART Recovery? Which is better suited to someone who doesn't like the spiritual aspect of AA?
I have a cocaine addiction and there are no medication treatments available. What evidence-based behavioral approaches work best for stimulant addiction?
I notice I compulsively scroll social media for hours even when I don't want to. Can technology and phone use become a true addiction?
I am in recovery and want to tell my employer about my history. Are there legal protections for people with addiction histories in the workplace?
